title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. erysibodes
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. erysibodes, also known as Rough Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues and reduce the risk of infection. The plant's leaves, flowers, and roots can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that offer various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating menstrual cramps, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. erysibodes can be consumed internally or applied topically to the skin to take advantage of its medicinal and therapeutic properties.
